title: E. Miller Christen
text:
I am a graduate student in Statistics at the University of South Carolina with a background in mathematical sciences and physics. My research interests focus on machine learning and statistical modeling to study galaxy morphology classification. I’m passionate about astrostatistics, big data, and applying statistical methods to solve real-world problems.

Learn more about me →

title: Galaxy Morphology
text: This is an interactive visualization of learned feature representations from a CvT-13 model fine-tuned on Galaxy Zoo 2 images. The target data here are unlabeled JWST Advanced Early Extragalactic Survey (JADES) cutouts generated by stacked NIRCam filters and segmentation mask available at: https://archive.stsci.edu/hlsp/jades. Explore how the model clusters different galaxy morphologies (round, in-between, cigar-shaped, edge-on, spiral) in embedding space, with each point representing a galaxy’s compressed visual features. The remaining unconfident set of images are left for expert review to filter point sources or discover additional morphology classes.

title: E. Miller Christen
text:
Experience
University of South Carolina Department of Statistics | August 2025 – Present

Developed a CvT-based model for galaxy morphology classification using JWST Advanced Deep Extragalactic Survey images.

Clemson University Department of Mathematics | August 2023 – May 2024

Analyzed the behavior of Bounded Height Matrices of Integers (Bohemian Matrices) using Singular Value Decomposition (SVD) of eigenvalue density plots.

Clemson University Laboratory for Band Engineering of Quantum Systems | August 2023 – May 2024

Designed and tested instrumentation for an electron radiation chamber (including a 3d printed einzel lens, UHV cryocooler, and cold finger shield)

University of Notre Dame Nuclear Science Laboratory | Summer 2023

Optimized modeling of half-life curves through statistical analysis of \(^{33}\text{Cl}\) decay data.

title: E. Miller Christen
text:

University of South Carolina | Graduate Assistant | 2024 – 2026
- Lecturer and Lab Instructor for Elementary Statistics (STAT 201).
Association of Women in Mathematics (AWM) | President | 2023 – 2024
- Led Clemson University student chapter, managing budgets and coordinating events.
Melton Observatory | Volunteer | 2025 – 2026
- Assist with public viewing sessions and telescope alignment and operation.
